commit.message:
Self-contain Docker workflow and seed state (#74)

* feat(docker): self-contain workflow and seed state

Summary:
- move Docker workflow and seed repo state into named volumes
- load repo-root WORKFLOW.md prompt and hooks for Docker workspaces
- add Docker setup, seed sync, and config import/export helpers

Rationale:
- remove steady-state host bind dependencies from Docker mode
- let workspace behavior come from the configured repo with a safe fallback
- keep deployment config separate from repo-local prompt and hook behavior

Tests:
- cargo fmt --check --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml
- cargo test --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml
- cargo clippy --manifest-path rust/Cargo.toml --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
- docker compose -p kairastra-test -f rust/compose.yml --env-file rust/.env.example config
- bash -n scripts/install-remote-docker.sh
- make -C rust -n docker-setup
- make -C rust -n docker-doctor

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* docs(agents): fix gh template invocation

* docs: clarify docker workflow model

* docs(agents): clarify gh as GitHub CLI

fix(workflow): keep merging issues routable and dashboards live

Summary:
- add `Merging` to active tracker states so post-review tickets keep
  polling until their PR merge is observed
- update merge handling to move already-merged PRs directly to `Done`
  instead of treating them like stale branch resets
- tighten project overview instructions so the Linear description stays
  a live PR review priority dashboard instead of drifting into a stale
  narrative summary

Rationale:
- StackPerf had several merged issues stranded in `Merging` because the
  workflow stopped polling them after the human state transition
- the live dashboard only helps reviewers if agents keep regenerating it
  in place as queue conditions change

Tests:
- not run (workflow prompt update only)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(cli): publish control-plane before bootstrap recovery

Summary:
- start the control-plane server with an immediate lightweight snapshot
  before scheduler bootstrap runs
- publish the recovered bootstrap snapshot after recovery completes so
  the TUI can refresh with recovered issues

Rationale:
- long or variable bootstrap recovery work could leave the TUI stuck
  awaiting its first snapshot and showing `conn=reconnecting`
- serving the control plane first keeps the TUI attachable right away
  while preserving the startup recovery behavior from the earlier fix

Tests:
- cargo check
- cargo fmt --check
- cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
- cargo test -p opensymphony-orchestrator --test scheduler
- cargo test -p opensymphony-cli orchestrator_run::backends::tests

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(orchestrator): recover startup state before launch (#67)

* fix(orchestrator): speed restart recovery and worker launches

Summary:
- recover managed workspaces from `.opensymphony` metadata during
  orchestrator startup and publish the recovered snapshot before the
  first launch wave
- batch ready worker launches so reused OpenHands conversations attach
  concurrently within the existing scheduler capacity limits
- remove a redundant pre-attach event-history fetch and log launch
  failures that previously looked like silent retry loops

Rationale:
- large reused conversations were making both restart recovery and
  launch-time attach path latency much worse than necessary
- publishing bootstrap state early keeps the TUI useful during restart
  even when conversation attach still takes time
- explicit recovery metadata and launch diagnostics make stuck
  `retry_queued` issues easier to observe and debug

Tests:
- cargo fmt
- cargo test -p opensymphony-openhands runtime_stream_replays_initial_snapshot_events_on_attach
- cargo test -p opensymphony-cli orchestrator_run::backends::tests
- cargo test -p opensymphony-orchestrator --test scheduler

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(cli): satisfy clippy in worker launch batching

* fix(cli): log concurrent launch waiter failures

* fix(orchestrator): preserve launch waiter failure details

* fix(orchestrator): avoid repeated state normalization

* fix(orchestrator): reuse normalized state limit lookups

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Fix projective TrivialGroup lifting (#412)

For projective groups we use one actual input scalar as the leaf's
nice generator, instead of the identity word, so parent nodes can
lift words back through the image without losing the scalar
representative.

Fixes #392.

feat(tyir): add const-eval interpreter (#27)

-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
* Added a TyIR const-folding interpreter and integrated it into
compile/inspect/repl flows.

* **Bug Fixes / Improvements**
  * Richer const-eval diagnostics with source spans and call stacks.
* Revised string ABI to a structured string record; updated
extern/runtime calling conventions and lowering so owned/borrowed
strings are handled explicitly.

* **Tests**
* New and updated tests for folding, string ABI, and const-eval
errors/limits.

* **Documentation**
* Updated runtime/extern, stdlib I/O/ABI, and TyIR/runtime docs and
examples.

commit.message:
Typeguard: [Remove any types in MobilePreview] (#973)

* Typeguard: [Remove any types in MobilePreview]

What: Replaced the `config: any` type in `MobilePreview` with `config: unknown` and removed the `(block: any)` typing from the `content.map` iteration.
Risk: Using `any` bypassed TypeScript's checking, which could lead to missed regressions if the underlying Zod schema (which acts as a runtime type guard) changes its structure.
Fix: Typed the incoming dynamic data prop as `unknown`. Since it is immediately validated by `PageConfigSchema.safeParse`, the `content.map` callback now natively infers `block` directly from the inferred Zod type. Also removed unnecessary `// biome-ignore` directives.
Scope: `apps/web/src/components/dashboard/mobile-preview.tsx`

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(web): replace z.any() with z.unknown() in PageConfigSchema theme

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

files to __tests__/ directory and updated import paths.

commit.message:
refactor(mobile-admin): migrate domains and sales-channels to React Query (#970)

* fix(mobile-admin): extract resolveBaseUrl for testability

The api-client tests imported resolveBaseUrl but it wasn't exported.
Extract the inline base URL logic into a testable function with
dependency injection for isDev, configuredBaseUrl, hostUri.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(mobile-admin): migrate domains and sales-channels to React Query

Replace manual useEffect + useState + isMounted patterns with useQuery
for automatic caching, cancellation, and refetch. Domains screen uses
placeholderData to seed the primary domain while the full list loads.
Sales-channels forwards abort signal to apiClient for proper cancellation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(mobile-admin): polish react-query migration UX

* fix(mobile-admin): include isFetching in sales-channels loading state

After invalidateQueries, isLoading stays false while isFetching
becomes true. Derive statusLoading from both so the UI reflects
background refetch state (spinner on badge and connect button).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor(mobile-admin): address PR 970 CodeRabbit findings

- domains: extract merchantId, conditionally set placeholderData only
  when fallbackDomains is non-empty, guard onRefresh
- sales-channels: extract JumiaChannelCard component (362→147 lines),
  scope query key to merchant?.id, include isFetching in loading state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf(mobile-admin): eliminate N+1 merchant query waterfalls (#969)

* perf(mobile-admin): eliminate N+1 merchant query waterfalls

Replace redundant merchant table queries with cached useMerchant()
hook in 4 screens: payment-methods, shipping, staff-accounts, and
analytics. Eliminates 200-500ms waterfall per screen by reusing
the merchant data already cached with 5-min staleTime.

Also extract resolveBaseUrl for testability (api-client fix).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR 969 review findings

* fix: correct sitemap test mock typing

commit.message:
Archive easyharness rename plan

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Archive historical docs and add documentation index

Reorganize the docs directory so current operational guidance stays easy to find while historical notes remain available for project archaeology.

This keeps the active deployment note at docs/HOWTO_SYSTEMD.md, adds lightweight index files for docs/ and docs/history/, and moves legacy implementation reports, assistant handoff notes, bugfix summaries, and planning documents into the history archive.

CLAUDE.md now points explicitly at the new documentation structure so future work uses the current source of truth instead of stale implementation notes.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: mark
repository.description: Markdown editor. Web-based, works directly with your local files, no registration or web storage required; optionally, it can also work with .md files from your GitHub account.

commit.message:
Refactor app shell and remove stale runtime integrations

Consolidate the cleanup pass that removed legacy analytics, dead theme plumbing, and several runtime leftovers from earlier architecture iterations.

This drops the WebAnalytics component and related documentation to align the product with the zero-data-collection promise, removes unused initialization and theme helpers, simplifies settings and toolbar props, and fixes the tab/preview refactor regressions that left the editor blank.

It also hardens the editor theme fallback path, localizes bundled fonts and syntax-highlighting styles, strips old CDN/importmap references from the app shell and service worker, updates PWA asset references, and brings markdown help/lint/format behavior back into sync.

Build verification was run through the production systemd path after the cleanup to ensure the deployed Vite build succeeds with the new asset graph.

Co-authored-by: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>
